description: Three consecutive GetById calls use the null-forgiving operator `!` to dereference the result directly (lines 57-59) without a prior null-check assertion. If GetById unexpectedly returns null the test throws NullReferenceException instead of a meaningful assertion failure. Compare with the same test class's other GetById usages that correctly call result.Should().NotBeNull() first.
suggestedFix: Store each result in a variable, assert NotBeNull(), then access properties via the null-forgiving operator: `var r1 = _sut.GetById(team1.Id); r1.Should().NotBeNull(); r1!.Name.Should().Be("Northford Athletic");` — or chain: `_sut.GetById(team1.Id).Should().NotBeNull().And.Match<Team>(t => t.Name == "Northford Athletic");`
